rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the two concrete human asks (fix precommit; run the diffusion benchmark script), so recall is low for both. Y engages more substantively with the actual GT concern (parent-first ordering of source_outputs) by tracing orchestrator.py construction and CFG-companion handling with specific line citations, giving it better recall/precision but landing mostly as confirmatory validation with few concrete asks (low actionability, verdict APPROVE). X offers several well-grounded, concretely actionable minor findings (docstring gaps, missing None-prompt test, hardcoded log index) that are genuinely useful but only tangentially touch the ordering concern and ignore the process asks entirely, and includes one vaguely-sourced claim ('CfgCompanionTracker') without citation.
